Question: What is the combined total yearly microbial respiration due to decomposition across these four peatland settings: the perched hollow central site experiencing periodic flooding from river backflows with iron-associated organic particle transport and slow-decaying dwarf shrub litter; the rewetted farm peatland with a persistent moisture fringed zone supporting methane-producing microbes, where organic matter seeps into intertwined stream channels from banks and woody plants are in a moderate stage of decay releasing lignin-rich detritus; and finally, at the coastal peat tongue, where a long water retention period under low evaporation feeds two different organic carbon transport pathways—one through underground fen transition water flow associated with a persistent sedge mat low in nitrogen, and the other via surface runoff through Sphagnum-based litter allowing fen sedge with irregular senescence waves?
SQL Query: SELECT SUM(AnnualHeterotrophicRespiration) AS AnnualHeterotrophicRespiration FROM (SELECT AnnualHeterotrophicRespiration AS AnnualHeterotrophicRespiration FROM PeatlandCarbonLedger WHERE PeatBlockID = "PB-Depress-05 — perched hollow central" AND HydroseralResidence = "intermittent surface inundation driven by fluvial backwater" AND LateralDOCLeachingPathway = "colloidal transport associated with iron-organic complexes" AND VegetationSenescenceClass = "litter-accumulating dwarf shrub assemblage slow decomposition" UNION ALL SELECT AnnualHeterotrophicRespiration AS AnnualHeterotrophicRespiration FROM PeatlandCarbonLedger WHERE PeatBlockID = "PB-Reclaimed-16 — rewetted agricultural peat" AND HydroseralResidence = "persistent capillary fringe sustaining methanogenic microsites" AND LateralDOCLeachingPathway = "bank seepage to adjacent anastomosing stream channels" AND VegetationSenescenceClass = "woody shrub intermediate senescence and lignin-rich fallout" UNION ALL SELECT AnnualHeterotrophicRespiration AS AnnualHeterotrophicRespiration FROM PeatlandCarbonLedger WHERE PeatBlockID = "PB-Blanket-07 — coastal peat tongue" AND HydroseralResidence = "long-tail residence under low evapotranspirative flux" AND LateralDOCLeachingPathway = "subsurface matrix flow via fen transition lenses" AND VegetationSenescenceClass = "cyperaceae mat persistent senescence with low nitrogen content" UNION ALL SELECT AnnualHeterotrophicRespiration AS AnnualHeterotrophicRespiration FROM PeatlandCarbonLedger WHERE PeatBlockID = "PB-Blanket-07 — coastal peat tongue" AND HydroseralResidence = "long-tail residence under low evapotranspirative flux" AND LateralDOCLeachingPathway = "surficial overland flow through Sphagnum-derived litter" AND VegetationSenescenceClass = "fen sedge clumping with asynchronous senescence waves");

Question: What is the total greenhouse gas flux recorded across all data points that include (1) the transitional fen zone with vascular grasses in Forsinard Flows, Scotland measured during the 2018 monthly automated chambers, where methane moves through plant tissues, (2) the partially drained and re-wetted peat with early grass succession at Mer Bleue Bog, Canada, also from the 2018 monthly automated chambers, where carbon release mainly comes from aerobic surface decomposition, (3) the raised hummock area dominated by Sphagnum moss and dwarf shrubs at Forsinard measured in the 2021 multi-season campaign showing nitrous oxide emissions linked to denitrification during drying periods, and (4) the peat plateau zone with oxygen-exposed surface and root layers in the Sundarbans mangrove transect, Bangladesh, sampled in 2023 with day and night contrasts, where methane uptake is driven by oxidation at aerobic lawns?
SQL Query: SELECT SUM(GreenhouseFluxValue) AS GreenhouseFluxValue FROM (SELECT GreenhouseFluxValue AS GreenhouseFluxValue FROM PeatlandFluxInventory WHERE SiteID = "Forsinard Flows research bog (Scotland)" AND BiomeMicrofacet = "transitional lagg fen with vascular graminoid mosaics" AND MeasurementInterval = "2018 high-frequency automated chamber campaign (monthly composites)" AND ProcessDominantPathway = "plant-mediated methane transport via aerenchyma and stemflow" UNION ALL SELECT GreenhouseFluxValue AS GreenhouseFluxValue FROM PeatlandFluxInventory WHERE SiteID = "Mer Bleue Bog Research Site (Canada)" AND BiomeMicrofacet = "drained/re-wetted peat segment exhibiting early successional graminoids" AND MeasurementInterval = "2018 high-frequency automated chamber campaign (monthly composites)" AND ProcessDominantPathway = "aerobic heterotrophic respiration from surface peat and litter oxidation" UNION ALL SELECT GreenhouseFluxValue AS GreenhouseFluxValue FROM PeatlandFluxInventory WHERE SiteID = "Forsinard Flows research bog (Scotland)" AND BiomeMicrofacet = "hummock microrelief dominated by Sphagnum and ericaceous dwarf-shrub community" AND MeasurementInterval = "2021 multi-season campaign with winter-spring and late-summer samplings" AND ProcessDominantPathway = "coupled denitrification-N2O transient emissions during drawdown" UNION ALL SELECT GreenhouseFluxValue AS GreenhouseFluxValue FROM PeatlandFluxInventory WHERE SiteID = "Sundarbans mangrove-peat transect (Bangladesh)" AND BiomeMicrofacet = "peat plateau with aerobic surface peat and root-litter horizon" AND MeasurementInterval = "2023 nested plot campaign with diel sampling and wet-dry contrasts" AND ProcessDominantPathway = "oxidation-dominated CH4 sink at aerobic lawn surfaces");

Question: Considering the measurements from the following four specific samples — (1) SUBH-PEA-0101 basal peat sequence sample in the paludified black spruce swamp transition zone with vegetation "Eriophorum vaginatum tussock carpet with sparse trees" measured in the 2022 hydrological rebound monitoring year; (2) HOL-PEA-0001 stratigraphic profile A (peat core accession) in the microtopographic tussock-hollow assemblage with vegetation "Calluna vulgaris heath mosaic on degraded peat surface" measured in the 2015 fiscal year resurvey of reference plots; (3) HOL-PEA-0003 stratigraphic profile C - transect 1 in the rhynchospora-dominated lagg zone adjacent minerogenic soil with vegetation "Eriophorum vaginatum tussock carpet with sparse trees" measured in the 2016 baseline plot census and stratigraphy; and (4) HOL-PEA-0001 stratigraphic profile A (peat core accession) in the cryptic inundation fringe beneath canopy gaps with vegetation "Empetrum-nitrogen-limited dwarf shrub heath overlay" measured in the 2018 eddy-covariance flux year — what is the total peat heterotrophic respiration (peat carbon released through microbial respiration) summed across these four samples?
SQL Query: SELECT SUM(PeatHeterotrophicRespiration) AS PeatHeterotrophicRespiration FROM (SELECT PeatHeterotrophicRespiration AS PeatHeterotrophicRespiration FROM PeatlandRespirationRegistry WHERE ChronostratigraphicTag = "SUBH-PEA-0101 basal peat sequence sample" AND HydrologicalFaciesZone = "paludified black spruce swamp transition zone" AND VegetationFunctionalAssemblage = "Eriophorum vaginatum tussock carpet with sparse trees" AND MeasurementEpoch = "2022 hydrological rebound monitoring year" UNION ALL SELECT PeatHeterotrophicRespiration AS PeatHeterotrophicRespiration FROM PeatlandRespirationRegistry WHERE ChronostratigraphicTag = "HOL-PEA-0001 stratigraphic profile A (peat core accession)" AND HydrologicalFaciesZone = "microtopographic tussock-hollow assemblage" AND VegetationFunctionalAssemblage = "Calluna vulgaris heath mosaic on degraded peat surface" AND MeasurementEpoch = "2015 fiscal year resurvey of reference plots" UNION ALL SELECT PeatHeterotrophicRespiration AS PeatHeterotrophicRespiration FROM PeatlandRespirationRegistry WHERE ChronostratigraphicTag = "HOL-PEA-0003 stratigraphic profile C - transect 1" AND HydrologicalFaciesZone = "rhynchospora-dominated lagg zone adjacent minerogenic soil" AND VegetationFunctionalAssemblage = "Eriophorum vaginatum tussock carpet with sparse trees" AND MeasurementEpoch = "2016 baseline plot census and stratigraphy" UNION ALL SELECT PeatHeterotrophicRespiration AS PeatHeterotrophicRespiration FROM PeatlandRespirationRegistry WHERE ChronostratigraphicTag = "HOL-PEA-0001 stratigraphic profile A (peat core accession)" AND HydrologicalFaciesZone = "cryptic inundation fringe beneath canopy gaps" AND VegetationFunctionalAssemblage = "Empetrum-nitrogen-limited dwarf shrub heath overlay" AND MeasurementEpoch = "2018 eddy-covariance flux year");

Question: What is the combined total carbon dioxide release rate, in g CO2 m-2 day-1, obtained by summing the FluxMagnitude values recorded at (1) the EDC-MX07 montane grassland flux mast station for the seasonally inundated floodplain willow carr with hydric soils where canopy respiration diurnal hysteresis was measured by branch chambers during the instrument intercomparison 2020 cross-protocol round-robin; (2) the EDC-PH03 temperate peatland micrometeorological node array for the climax old-growth conifer stand with deep organic horizon where lateral dissolved organic carbon export acted as a fluvial CO2 source during the summer 2019 isotope-tracer pulse-chase deployment; (3) the EDC-AR11 arctic polygon tundra eddy station cluster for the aggrading pasture-to-bog transition with Sphagnum co-dominance where sediment porewater CO2 release was measured during tidal exposure cycles in the instrument intercomparison 2020 cross-protocol round-robin; and (4) the EDC-PH03 temperate peatland micrometeorological node array for the heathland with ericaceous dwarf-shrub dominance and thin peat where soil surface diffusive CO2 efflux was measured via automated chamber array during the growing-season 2022 high-frequency eddy covariance campaign?
